summary: Scorching Jack Straw, fiery Ship of Fools, and tight Playing in the Band jam
blurb: The show shines on a blistering Jack Straw and an incendiary Playing in the Band sequence that rolls into Shakedown Street. Jerry’s guitar is on fire on Ship of Fools while the 1978 drum crew stays tidy and lively. Overall a solid second set despite a modest first‑set flow.
review: December 13, 1978 at Curtis Hixon Convention Hall captured a Dead show that still feels fresh after all these years. The first set drifts through familiar territory – Passenger, They Love Each Other and a decent Candyman – but the real fireworks arrive in the second half. Jerry Garcia erupts on Ship of Fools (“on fire”) and launches an outrageous Playing in the Band jam that rockets into a space‑filled Shakedown Street, delivering the kind of high‑energy improvisation fans crave. The drum duo of Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart is described as tidy and lively, laying down a rock‑solid foundation for the soaring jams. While the SBD from Hinko has a warm, slightly gritty character that some call “beat” or “dirtier,” its clarity lets the band’s dynamics shine through. Even the audience tape picks up the spacey vibe of set two, confirming this show’s strength lies in its second‑set momentum and fiery moments rather than first‑set fireworks.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two of the three recordings convey clear positive sentiment, highlighting Jack Straw, Playing in the Band, Ship of Fools and strong drum work. The third recording is mixed but only notes poor audio quality, not performance flaws. No consistent negative criticism of the playing appears, so a moderate positive adjustment (+0.8) is applied.

best_recording.reason: Warm, slightly gritty soundboard that remains clear and captures the band’s energy and standout jams.

key_highlights: Scorching Jack Straw that kicks off a high‑octane second set
Jerry’s “on fire” performance on Ship of Fools and the ensuing Playing in the Band jam
Tidy, lively drumming from Bill and Mickey driving the Shakedown Street groove
Warm SBD sound that preserves the band’s dynamics without excessive glisten

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia is on fire on Ship of Fools and leads an outrageous Playing in the Band jam, delivering high‑energy guitar throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Phil stays nice and present in the mix, anchoring the low end solidly.
band_performance.Brent: Keith Godchaux provides a better‑than‑usual night with tasteful fills that complement the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey Hart contributes tidy, lively drumming that fuels the second‑set momentum.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann pairs with Mickey to lay down a strong rhythmic foundation throughout.
